segments.text:
Hi, I'm Robert Curlee,
technical product marketing manager here at Sonar.

And today I'm going to show you a cool new
capability in SonarQube Server,
which is our integration with Slack.

And this is just one of the few integrations
that we've added recently
in our 2026.1 LTA release.
This is a really powerful integration

because you can stay working in Slack
or in the tools that you normally use
on a regular basis.

You don't have to leave those tools
to go find out the status of your code
and the changes in your quality gates.

So here I have a project in SonarQube
and I'm just showing you what I did.

I've got a failed quality gate here
in my demo project
because I created an issue on purpose.

Which is I embedded a hardcoded token
in my code.

And of course that's going to trigger
a secrets detection issue
and it's going to fail my quality gate.
But the cool thing that happened is

I've got an integration with my Slack
and I created a channel
with that integration.
And now in Slack I can see that

when I created that issue
and ran an analysis
and the quality gate failed,
it shows me now that the gate failed
and it had previously passed.

It shows me the project
and where the issue occurred.

And I can click on this
and drill into SonarQube
if I want to go resolve the issue.
Again, I just wanted to show you this

because I think it's a really fun
and cool way to work.

A lot of people work with Slack
integrations and notifications today.
And I hope you enjoy it and use it.

fullText:
Hi, I'm Robert Curlee,
technical product marketing manager here at Sonar. And today I'm going to show you a cool new
capability in SonarQube Server, which is our integration with Slack. And this is just one of the few integrations
that we've added recently
in our 2026.1 LTA release. This is a really powerful integration because you can stay working in Slack
or in the tools that you normally use
on a regular basis. You don't have to leave those tools
to go find out the status of your code and the changes in your quality gates. So here I have a project in SonarQube
and I'm just showing you what I did. I've got a failed quality gate here
in my demo project because I created an issue on purpose. Which is I embedded a hardcoded token
in my code. And of course that's going to trigger
a secrets detection issue and it's going to fail my quality gate. But the cool thing that happened is I've got an integration with my Slack
and I created a channel
with that integration. And now in Slack I can see that when I created that issue
and ran an analysis
and the quality gate failed, it shows me now that the gate failed and it had previously passed. It shows me the project
and where the issue occurred. And I can click on this
and drill into SonarQube
if I want to go resolve the issue. Again, I just wanted to show you this because I think it's a really fun
and cool way to work. A lot of people work with Slack
integrations and notifications today. And I hope you enjoy it and use it.
